description: Islamic theology and belief — Tawhid, pillars of Iman, attributes of Allah, shirk, predestination

questions.question: What are the six pillars of Iman (faith) in Islam? Cite the hadith of Jibreel.
What is Tawhid? Explain its three categories with Quranic evidence.
What does the Quran say about shirk (associating partners with Allah)? Cite exact verses.
Explain the concept of Qadar (divine decree) — its four levels.
What does the Quran say about the attributes of Allah? Cite Surah Al-Ikhlas and Ayat al-Kursi.
What are the Angels (Mala'ikah) in Islam? Name at least 5 with their duties, citing relevant hadith.
What are the revealed Books of Allah that Muslims must believe in? Cite Quranic evidence.
Explain belief in the Day of Judgement (Yawm al-Qiyamah) with Quranic references.
What is bid'ah (religious innovation) in Islam? What did the Prophet ﷺ say about it?
What does Islam say about intercession (shafa'a)? Who can intercede on the Day of Judgement?
What is the Islamic belief regarding the Prophets and Messengers? How many are mentioned in the Quran?
Explain the concept of Tawakkul (reliance on Allah) with Quran and Hadith.
What is the Islamic concept of the soul (Ruh)? What does the Quran say about its nature?
What are the 99 Names of Allah (Asmaul Husna)? Give the first 20 with Arabic and meanings.
What does Islam say about the punishment of the grave (Azab al-Qabr)? Cite relevant hadiths.
Explain Al-Wala wal-Bara (loyalty and disavowal) in Islamic aqeedah.
What is the Islamic view of Jesus (Isa عليه السلام)? How does Islam differ from Christianity?
What does the Quran say about creation of the heavens and earth? Cite the relevant verses.
Explain the concept of Tawbah (repentance) in Islam. What are its conditions?
What is the Islamic belief about Paradise (Jannah)? Describe it with Quranic verses.
